steps.title: Use the axis of symmetry shortcut to find a
steps.content: $f(-12) = f(4)$ means these two $x$-values are symmetric about the vertex. The axis is at $\frac{-12 + 4}{2} = -4$. For $f(x) = ax^2 + 5x + c$, the vertex formula gives $x = -\frac{b}{2a} = -\frac{5}{2a}$. Setting equal: $-\frac{5}{2a} = -4 \Rightarrow a = \frac{5}{8}$. So Statement II ($a \geq 1$) is false.

steps.title: Check whether c < 0 must hold
steps.content: The vertex value is $k = f(-4) = \frac{5}{8}(16) + 5(-4) + c = 10 - 20 + c = c - 10$. The parabola opens upward ($a \gt 0$) and the problem requires $k \lt 0$, so $c - 10 \lt 0 \Rightarrow c \lt 10$. A value like $c = 1 \gt 0$ is perfectly valid, so Statement I ($c \lt 0$) is not necessarily true. Neither statement must hold. D is correct.
